steps.title: Convert line r to slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Starting with $4x - 3y = 8$, isolate $y$ to find the slope.
$$-3y = -4x + 8$$
$$y = \frac{4}{3}x - \frac{8}{3}$$
The slope of line $r$ is $\frac{4}{3}$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines rule
steps.content: Parallel lines have the same slope. Since line $s$ is parallel to line $r$, the slope of line $s$ equals the slope of line $r$, which is $\frac{4}{3}$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
